ProjectsScreen.title: Projects

paragraphs: A selection of shipped products and sites — not an exhaustive list.
Each project is chosen to show product thinking, clear architecture, or demanding frontend execution.

ProjectsScreen.selectionNote: Curation is part of perceived level: quality, intent, and coherence over volume.

meta.title: Projects
meta.description: Selected case studies and shipped work — Estime Vangu.

fleetscan.name: FleetScan
fleetscan.tagline: Fleet incident reporting via QR and license plate
fleetscan.summary: Mobile-first field tool to scan a truck and report fleet status — damage, compliance, manager overview.

Camera QR scan and normalized plate input
PostgreSQL RLS and Zod validation at boundaries

shadow.name: Shadow
shadow.tagline: Private messaging, shared calendar, and PWA
shadow.summary: Installable app for a closed circle: ephemeral messages, groups, events, and notifications.

Full PWA with service worker
Security, architecture, and pre-deploy audit docs

fleetscan.name: FleetScan
fleetscan.tagline: Fleet incidents, field scan, manager view
fleetscan.role: Product design, frontend architecture, Supabase integration, auth, and scan flows.

meta.title: FleetScan — Case study
meta.description: Fleet incident business app — QR, roles, RLS, and validation.

problem: Field teams need to report truck status in seconds, without friction or input errors.
Managers need a reliable fleet view without heavy tools or shared spreadsheets.
approach: Mobile-first: scan QR or plate, vehicle sheet, binary damage / nothing-to-report choice.
Minimal backend via Supabase: auth, role RLS, versioned migrations, strict server validation.

context.title: Product context
paragraphs: FleetScan (scratch_tracker repo) serves real depot usage: speed, clarity, few taps.
MVP covers driver journey and staff overview; roadmap documents photos, Realtime, and advanced cache.

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: Route groups `(splash)`, `(public)`, `(protected)` with clear responsibility split.
Feature / controller / view pattern for scan, truck dashboard, and fleet overview.
bullets: lib/: Zod validation, server actions, plate and date mappers

decisions.title: Key decisions
paragraphs: fr / nl / en i18n via cookie — stable URLs for multi-country field use.
Zod at boundaries: external data is untrusted by default.
bullets: No over-abstraction: one function per file, isolated features
Camera scan isolated in dedicated feature
Shared RLS documented in SQL migrations

execution.title: Execution & next
paragraphs: Foundation ready for production: tests, product docs, scalable structure.
Next: field screenshots, final create flow, Vercel deploy and monitoring.

shadow.name: Shadow
shadow.tagline: Private comms and calendar for a closed group
shadow.role: Fullstack on Next.js and Supabase — messaging, calendar, PWA, and security hardening.

meta.title: Shadow — Case study
meta.description: Private messaging + calendar app — realtime, PWA, RLS, and E2E tests.

problem: A closed group needs a private space: disappearing messages, shared calendar, without public tools.
The experience must work on mobile, install as an app, and stay performant.
approach: Next.js + Supabase stack: SSR auth, modular CRUD, realtime for badges and threads.
Production PWA with manifest, service worker, and explicit middleware exclusions.

context.title: Context
paragraphs: Shadow combines ephemeral messaging (24h), direct and group chats, and an events calendar.
14 versioned SQL migrations; docs cover architecture, security, and pre-deploy audit.

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: Strict features/ split: auth, messages, calendar, home.
lib/supabase/CRUD/ per entity — readability and testability.
bullets: SWR for message cache and UI reactivity
getClaims() middleware and auth rate limit

decisions.title: Decisions
paragraphs: RLS as main barrier — documented pitfalls (e.g. conversations).
Framer Motion with useReducedMotion on sensitive interactions.
bullets: Explicit webpack for PWA / Vercel compatibility
Bundle analyzer to keep perf in check

execution.title: Execution
paragraphs: Advanced MVP ready for final hardening (CSP, error boundaries flagged in audit).
Strong proof of a complete technical product, not only a marketing site.

jikowood.name: Jikowood
jikowood.role: Lead frontend — architecture, gallery, motion, i18n, and quality (E2E tests).

meta.title: Jikowood — Case study
meta.description: Premium craftsman redesign — immersive gallery, Lenis, tests, and conventions.

problem: A premium craftsman needs a showcase matching the work — not a flat catalog site.
Project navigation must feel fluid and memorable on mobile without hurting performance.
approach: Typed static project data — 5 showcases, ~40 visuals.
Fullscreen gallery with crossfade and pure functions for prev/next navigation.

context.title: Context
paragraphs: Full redesign (jikowood_refonte) with ARCHITECTURE-CONVENTIONS and ROADMAP docs.
Zod-validated contact form — full UI, server send still to wire.

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: features/gallery with dedicated README; dynamic [slug]/[projectSlug] routes.
lib/gallery/compute-* for navigation logic without side effects.

decisions.title: Decisions
paragraphs: Lenis for premium scroll with reduced-motion fallback.
No CMS: full control over visual quality and pacing.

execution.title: Execution
paragraphs: Ideal to show frontend craft + test discipline on a marketing site.
Photo assets already in repo — mainly need production captures for portfolio.

portfolio-pro.name: Portfolio Pro
portfolio-pro.role: Product design, architecture, motion system, i18n, and design system — solo developer.

meta.title: Portfolio Pro — Case study
meta.description: Building a premium portfolio — motion, product strategy, and narrative shell.

problem: A generic portfolio does not prove product thinking or the ability to ship a coherent shell.
A premium, mobile-first experience was needed without cloning an Awwwards template.
approach: Phased strategy documented before content (identity, UX, motion, architecture).
Modular GSAP: one animation function per file, centralized timings, reduced motion everywhere.

context.title: Intent
paragraphs: Frontend-first, product-oriented positioning — measured fullstack.
Phase 1 shipped: splash, hero, profile manifesto, animated nav; projects and contact in progress.

architecture.title: Architecture
paragraphs: features/ for screens, components/ for UI, lib/animation/ for motion (41 modules).
Cookie i18n without /fr prefix — stable URLs, opaque locale.
bullets: Theme: GSAP interpolation on 14 CSS variables
Strict ESLint: no any, restricted unknown

decisions.title: Decisions
paragraphs: No Framer Motion — GSAP consistency across the shell.
Profile structured in 6 product sections instead of HTML bio.
bullets: next/og generated favicon aligned with brand
Imposed CSS tokens — no ad hoc colors
Project inventory as content source of truth

execution.title: Next
paragraphs: Integrate case studies and assets (screenshots, videos).
Vercel deploy and real contact — the shell is the execution proof.
I build modern web applications with a product-oriented frontend approach.
I mainly work on SaaS, B2B, and internal tools where frontend execution directly shapes day-to-day use.

productMindset.title: Product mindset
intro: Understand the real need before the technical solution. Decide early, ship focused MVPs, keep a clear trajectory.
No superfluous features: a foundation that can grow without over-complicating too soon.
productMindset.outro: Every decision should answer a concrete use case, not a feature checklist.

frontendPhilosophy.title: Frontend philosophy
intro: Frontend sets a product’s rhythm, fluidity, and perception — not just the screen.
frontendPhilosophy.outro: Interfaces that read quickly and move without friction.

buildingApplications.title: What I build
intro: Tools built for real contexts: business workflows, daily use, product logic behind the screen.
buildingApplications.outro: Real-world usage guides what we ship, not the other way around.

technicalApproach.title: Technical approach
intro: Simple, component-oriented architectures with coherent UI systems.
technicalApproach.outro: Technology should serve the product, not the other way around.

motionInteraction.title: Motion & interaction
intro: Animation and micro-interactions for hierarchy and fluidity — never for decoration.
motionInteraction.outro: Discreet, useful motion aligned with the rest of the interface.

collaborationExecution.title: Collaboration & execution
intro: Clear communication, sharp priorities, concrete progress.
collaborationExecution.outro: Serious products, well scoped, shipped cleanly.

closing.label: In short
paragraphs: SaaS, B2B, and modern tools with real product thinking.
Products built to last, evolve, and stay simple to use every day.

meta.title: Profile
meta.description: Product approach, frontend philosophy, and execution — Estime Vangu, product-oriented frontend engineer.
